comparison_serial: Below are two sets of serial {lang} code that we want to compare their differences. The first is labeled SOURCE CODE and it is followed by its output to stdout when it was executed as well as the total execution time. The second is labeled REFACTORED CODE followed by its output to stdout when executed on the same CPU system and its execution time measurement. Both codes are expected to perform the same function. Both codes are run on the same CPU system. However, the REFACTORED CODE is a refactoring of the SOURCE CODE generated by an LLM that was prompted to improve the runtime performance of the SOURCE CODE by reducing execution time while keeping the code strictly serial. Please compare the differences in the REFACTORED CODE from the SOURCE CODE by highlighting any changes in the REFACTORED CODE that might be related to improving its runtime performance. Avoid repeating the entire code for reference. Conclude your response with a simple list of the optimization methods, if any, used in the REFACTORED CODE not included in the SOURCE CODE that improves runtime performance. Surround this list of optimization methods with the tags [OPTIMIZATION LIST] and [/OPTIMIZATION LIST]
